comment: Cejel OSS leaderboard corpus: a one-run-sized elite set (~17 repositories) across web frameworks, build tooling, libraries, and a supply-chain-governance peer, plus this repository itself (alfred, and the public cejel CLI at packages/witan-cli) for transparency. Sizing rule: the corpus must be small enough that a full clone -> score -> render -> commit run completes synchronously inside one dispatch watchdog window with margin — growth happens by deliberate, regenerated additions, never by outrunning a single run (the 44-entry v2 corpus stranded a stale 6-repo board exactly this way). Selection rules: elite, widely-known projects only; permissive OSI licenses preferred and labeled per entry; behemoth trees the archetype detector cannot meaningfully score in one pass (linux, chromium, kubernetes-scale) are deliberately excluded and a per-repo clone timeout + maxTreeMB guard keeps any one repo from stalling a run. Every entry is published on the board regardless of how it scores, including any repo that outscores us; a repo that fails to clone or score is a loud ERROR row, never a silent drop. Entries are shallow-cloned (blobless, depth 1) read-only to be scored; none of their code is executed. The ranking is computed on the common externally-applicable dimension set (substrate-only dims excluded for everyone, including internal entries). Every entry also declares `visibility` (goal_cejel_redaction_policy_is_verifiability_2026-07-12): 'public' repositories cite full evidence paths and lines everywhere; 'private' repositories (this monorepo and its sub-packages) never cite a source path anywhere, in any field or format — the finding, its dimension, status, score, and content hash still stand; only the location is withheld.
